abstract: Using a supervised machine learning framework on a large training set of questions and answers, we identify 1,364 trigrams that signal nonanswers in earnings call questions and answers (Q&A). We show that this glossary has economic relevance by applying it to contemporaneous stock market reactions after earnings calls. Our findings suggest that obstructing the flow of information leads to significantly lower cumulative abnormal stock returns and higher implied volatility. As both our method and glossary are free of financial context, we believe that the measure is applicable to other fields with a Q&A setup outside the contextual domain of financial earnings conference calls.  This paper was accepted by Kay Giesecke, finance.  Supplemental Material: The data files are available at https://doi.org/10.1287/mnsc.2022.4597 .

ob.ai_reason: 监督ML框架识别财报电话非答案词汇，核心贡献为ML方法论
ob.tldr: 研究财报电话会议中"无实质回答"的量化方法。采用监督学习框架识别1364个三词组合特征作为"无效回答"信号，并应用于市场反应分析。核心发现：阻塞信息流通会导致股价累计异常收益显著降低、隐含波动率升高。该方法可迁移至其他Q&A场景。

title: “Let Me Get Back to You”—A Machine Learning Approach to Measuring NonAnswers
title_zh: "让我再回复你"——一种测量非答案的机器学习方法
